Get-ValidationRecipeCommandPlan maps the allowlist to reviewed argv builders, including the native desktop-editor shell recipe, the selected sample_desktop_runtime_game D3D12 environment fog package smoke desktop-runtime-sample-game-environment-fog-package, the selected sample_desktop_runtime_game D3D12 cloud layer package smoke desktop-runtime-sample-game-cloud-layer-package, the selected sample_desktop_runtime_game D3D12 rain precipitation package smoke desktop-runtime-sample-game-environment-precipitation-package, the selected sample_desktop_runtime_game cooked environment profile/index/scene dependency smoke desktop-runtime-sample-game-environment-profile-package, the selected 2D D3D12 performance baseline smoke installed-2d-performance-baseline-smoke, the selected 2D D3D12 short-soak long-run readiness smoke installed-2d-long-run-readiness-smoke, the opt-in host-owned 108000-frame soak host-2d-long-run-readiness-soak, the generated material/shader scaffold package recipe, the strict Vulkan variant desktop-runtime-generated-material-shader-scaffold-package-vulkan-strict, the strict Vulkan sample game UI atlas metadata package recipe desktop-runtime-sample-game-vulkan-ui-atlas-metadata-package, the Apple-host renderer Metal evidence recipe renderer-metal-apple-host-evidence, and the Windows-only dev preset lane dev-windows-editor-game-module-driver-load-tests (MK_editor_game_module_driver_load_tests). Invoke-ValidationRecipeCommandPlan executes reviewed argv entries with ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList; package-script recipes with hyphen-prefixed SmokeArgs use fixed reviewed PowerShell -Command wrappers so the array reaches tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 without evaluating raw manifest command strings. The runner is not arbitrary shell, and free-form arguments are rejected. It does not provide a CI service, package mutation, editor productization, shader graph/live shader generation, package streaming, native handle exposure, renderer/RHI precipitation execution, Metal readiness, Vulkan readiness by inference, broad environment readiness, broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization, broad long-running readiness, or general production renderer quality.

authoringSurfaces.notes: Headless, DesktopRuntimePackage, DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage, DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage, DesktopRuntime2DPackage, and DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds are validated by agent/static checks and focused desktop runtime/package recipes.

Game manifests expose backend readiness, importer requirements, packaging targets, runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, atlasTilemapAuthoringTargets, prefabScenePackageAuthoringTargets, registeredSourceAssetCookTargets, packageStreamingResidencyTargets, and validation recipes for generated games. runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows are descriptor-only inputs for validate-runtime-scene-package; atlasTilemapAuthoringTargets rows are deterministic data-only 2D tilemap package authoring descriptors; prefabScenePackageAuthoringTargets rows are deterministic 3D Scene/Prefab v2 plus selected SourceAssetRegistry package authoring descriptors; registeredSourceAssetCookTargets rows are descriptor-only cook-registered-source-assets inputs paired to prefab authoring rows with explicit or registry-closure dependency expansion; packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows are selected host-gated safe-point package streaming descriptors and do not add shell commands, runtime source parsing, renderer/RHI residency execution, broad background streaming, arbitrary eviction, editor productization, or native handles.
GUI-independent v0 authoring models exist, but they are not yet the future stable-id Scene/Component/Prefab Schema v2 command layer.

Contract-only stable-id scene/component/prefab v2 value APIs, validation, deterministic text IO, stable prefab override path composition, deterministic prefab source provenance rows through SceneNodePrefabSourceV2 / SceneComponentPrefabSourceV2 `prefab_source` text fields, non-mutating stable-id prefab instance refresh planning through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshPlanV2, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshRowV2, and plan_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_v2 comparing a selected instance root against PrefabDocumentV2 source_node_id / source_component_id rows, fail-closed duplicate_prefab_source_identity diagnostics for duplicate copied source identities, unsupported_nested_prefab_instance diagnostics for descendant nested prefab roots, unsupported_local_prefab_child / unsupported_local_prefab_component diagnostics for local selected-subtree rows without selected-prefab provenance, and pure value stable-id prefab instance refresh apply through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshResultV2 and apply_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_v2 are implemented in MK_scene. The value apply preserves matched node/component ids and state, adds deterministic instance_root/refresh/<source-id> rows, removes stale sourced rows, returns source-to-result mappings, and does not create editor actions, implement runtime prefab semantics, or perform nested propagation. Reviewed MK_tools dry-run/apply authoring commands now operate on this contract, instantiate-prefab records source prefab path plus source node/component ids, refresh-prefab-instance wraps the stable-id value apply into a reviewed safe .scene write surface, and the separate migrate-scene-v2-runtime-package surface bridges supported Scene v2 rows into the existing Scene v1 package update helper. A focused command-authored workflow proof now drives apply_source_asset_registration -> apply_registered_source_asset_cook_package -> apply_scene_v2_runtime_package_migration -> execute_runtime_scene_package_validation, including perspective camera and directional light cone-field projection. Source asset registration is ready through GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1. Editor migration, nested prefab propagation/merge resolution UX, broad/dependent package cooking, and 2D/3D vertical slices remain follow-up work.

Runtime Scene Gameplay Binding v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated gameplay authoring: RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingSourceRow rows carry binding_id, gameplay_system_id, slot_id, node_name, and a required RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingComponentKind, and resolve_runtime_scene_gameplay_bindings maps them to RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingRow node ids only when authored scene names are unique and required camera, light, mesh_renderer, sprite_renderer, or any_renderable component gates pass. Diagnostics cover invalid ids, duplicate binding ids, missing or duplicate node names, and missing required components; failed resolutions clear partial bindings. Selected 2D/3D package smokes expose gameplay_systems_scene_binding_ready and exact source/binding/system/component row counters before generated-game quality gates treat scene wiring as evidenced. This is not a gameplay system scheduler, script runtime, editor command surface, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Runtime Scene Gameplay Interaction Framework v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated gameplay authoring: RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionSourceRow rows carry action_id, RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionKind, source_binding_id, target_binding_id, objective_id, and amount; RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionPlanRequest carries the current RuntimeSceneGameplaySessionState; and plan_runtime_scene_gameplay_interactions consumes resolved RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingRow values to produce deterministic RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionPlan rows for trigger, pickup, damage, heal, objective_progress, objective_complete, win, loss, and restart interactions. Diagnostics cover invalid or duplicate action ids, missing source or target bindings, missing objective ids, invalid positive amount requirements, and rejected terminal-state transitions; failed plans clear partial rows and preserve the caller-provided session state. This is not a gameplay system scheduler, script runtime, physics/event dispatcher, editor command surface, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Runtime Session Profile Path Policy v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated game save/settings/profile placement: RuntimeSessionProfilePathRequest carries a safe game_id, profile_id, and project-relative root_path; plan_runtime_session_profile_paths returns deterministic save.gesave, settings.settings, and input.geinputprofile paths under one game-local profile root; and RuntimeSessionProfilePathPlan diagnostics fail closed for invalid game ids, profile ids, root paths, absolute paths, parent traversal, path separators in ids, and control characters. This is not a platform user-directory resolver, cloud save system, binary save format, native handle surface, filesystem mutation helper, or broad save migration/backup workflow.

Runtime Session Profile Document Bundle v1 layers deterministic save/settings/input-profile document load and reset/write helpers over Runtime Session Profile Path Policy v1: RuntimeSessionProfileDocuments carries RuntimeSaveData, RuntimeSettings, and RuntimeInputRebindingProfile values; load_runtime_session_profile_documents returns RuntimeSessionProfileDocumentRow rows with loaded, defaulted_missing, failed_corrupt, failed_unsupported_version, and failed_invalid_path statuses; and write_runtime_session_profile_documents validates all three documents before writing reviewed save.gesave, settings.settings, and input.geinputprofile defaults. Missing documents default without blocking, corrupt and unsupported-version documents block before gameplay startup, and unrelated files under the profile root are not deleted. This is not a cloud save system, binary save format, platform user-directory resolver, save migration/backup workflow, filesystem deletion helper, native handle surface, or game-specific save schema.

Runtime Session Profile Resume Plan v1 reviews already loaded profile documents before selected package resume claims: RuntimeSessionProfileResumeRequest carries a RuntimeSessionProfileDocumentLoadResult plus expected save slot, progression checkpoint, selected package id, and persisted profile id; RuntimeSessionProfileResumePlan reports status, loaded/defaulted document counts, save/settings schema versions, and RuntimeSessionProfileResumeDiagnostic rows; and plan_runtime_session_profile_resume fails closed for blocking document rows, missing save.slot/progression.checkpoint/package.id evidence, and mismatched save-slot/progression/package/profile values. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package expose selected --require-runtime-profile-resume counters including runtime_profile_resume_ready, runtime_profile_resume_loaded_documents, runtime_profile_resume_defaulted_documents, runtime_profile_resume_save_schema_version, and runtime_profile_resume_settings_schema_version. This is not a platform user-directory resolver, cloud save system, binary save format, save migration executor, filesystem mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific save schema beyond the reviewed resume evidence keys.

Runtime Simulation Persistence v1 reviews already-loaded RuntimeSessionProfileDocuments before simulation resume claims: RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRequest carries document load evidence, expected save slot/world id, current/minimum schema versions, required entity ids, supported migration steps, and key names; RuntimeSimulationPersistencePlan returns RuntimeSimulationPersistenceStatus, RuntimeSimulationPersistentEntityRow values from entity.<id>.(type|region|state_hash), RuntimeSimulationPersistenceMigrationStep rows, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnostic rows, and RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction recommendations; and plan_runtime_simulation_persistence fails closed for blocking save document rows, corrupt save documents, missing save.slot/world.id/snapshot.id/world.tick evidence, malformed or incomplete entity rows, missing required entities, unsupported schema versions, invalid migration rows, and missing migration-chain steps. Non-save profile document failures do not block valid save recovery. This is not a cloud save system, cross-device sync, multiplayer replication layer, binary compatibility policy, platform user-directory resolver, migration execution helper, filesystem/package mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific component serializer beyond reviewed save keys.

Runtime Menu HUD Intent Model v1 is a host-independent value contract for generated game HUD/menu intent: RuntimeMenuHudRowDesc rows carry label, counter, prompt, dialogue_box, input_binding_prompt, or command row intent; RuntimeMenuHudCommandIntent and RuntimeMenuHudCommandTarget encode pause/resume/restart/menu/custom commands before game code executes them; and plan_runtime_menu_hud returns deterministic RuntimeMenuHudDisplayRow and RuntimeMenuHudCommandRow rows only when validation succeeds. Diagnostics cover missing or duplicate row ids, unsupported row kinds, missing or duplicate command ids, invalid command intents, and invalid command targets; failed plans clear display and command rows. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package expose selected --require-runtime-menu-hud counters including runtime_menu_hud_ready, runtime_menu_hud_display_rows, runtime_menu_hud_command_rows, runtime_menu_hud_dialogue_rows, and runtime_menu_hud_input_binding_prompt_rows. This is not a widget toolkit, UI renderer, focus/navigation system, command execution dispatcher, full input rebinding panel, Dear ImGui/editor surface, platform UI service, native handle surface, or game-specific menu schema.

Runtime Gameplay Debug Overlay v1 is a host-independent value contract for generated gameplay diagnostics: RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRowDesc rows carry deterministic row id, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayCategory, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRowKind, label, value, highlighted, and visible fields for gameplay, input, audio, physics, navigation, AI, package, session, or custom rows; and plan_runtime_gameplay_debug_overlay returns RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayPlan rows only when validation succeeds. Diagnostics through RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayDiagnosticCode and RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayDiagnostic cover missing or duplicate row ids, missing labels, unsupported categories, and unsupported row kinds; failed plans clear overlay rows. sample_2d_playable_foundation adopts the planner for gameplay/input/audio/session rows and reports debug_overlay_rows=12 in the source-tree smoke. This is not a widget toolkit, UI renderer, renderer/RHI execution path, focus/navigation system, input binding system, debug command dispatcher, Dear ImGui/editor surface, platform UI service, native handle surface, telemetry backend, package streaming path, or game-specific debug schema.

Runtime Input Context Stack Planner v1 is a host-independent value contract for generated game input context handoff: RuntimeInputContextStackRequest carries ordered RuntimeInputContextLayerDesc rows for gameplay, menu, dialogue, rebinding, capture, or overlay layers; plan_runtime_input_context_stack validates context names, rejects duplicate contexts, returns a RuntimeInputContextStackPlan with a RuntimeInputContextStack only after diagnostics pass, supports default-context fallback, lets modal rows block lower-priority layers, and reports gameplay_input_available, gameplay_input_consumed, ui_context_active, and capture_context_active evidence. sample_2d_playable_foundation demonstrates source-tree adoption by planning a passive HUD overlay plus gameplay stack before evaluating RuntimeInputActionMap. Diagnostics cover invalid_context, duplicate_context, and no_active_context; failed plans clear partial stack rows. This is not a UI focus/navigation system, input binding document format change, command dispatcher, platform adapter, native handle surface, full runtime rebinding panel, or game-specific menu schema.

Runtime Script Sandbox Execution Facade v1 is a host-independent reviewed script dispatch contract for generated game-local scripts and mods: RuntimeScriptSandboxPolicyDesc carries module rows, entrypoints, requested permissions, reviewed host API allow-lists, per-entrypoint instruction and memory caps, and explicit replay seeds; plan_runtime_script_sandbox returns deterministic entrypoint and permission plan rows only when diagnostics pass; RuntimeScriptExecutionRequest plus execute_runtime_script_entrypoint dispatch one reviewed planned entrypoint through a caller-owned IRuntimeScriptExecutionAdapter. Execution diagnostics fail closed for missing plans, unplanned policies, missing entrypoints, replay seed mismatches, invalid or over-cap budgets, denied permission rows, adapter failures, and post-dispatch budget traps, and successful executions emit stats, host API call rows, output rows, dispatch evidence, and deterministic replay signatures. This is not a script interpreter, Lua/WASM/JIT runtime, native plugin ABI, filesystem/network/process adapter, package script execution path, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, editor command surface, or broad modding system.

Runtime Networking Foundation Policy v1 is a host-independent value contract for reviewed multiplayer intent: RuntimeNetworkFoundationPolicyDesc carries network session rows, reviewed transport capability rows, local role, trust boundary, replication channel declarations, and deterministic replay prerequisites; plan_runtime_network_foundation returns deterministic session, transport, channel, and replay prerequisite rows only when diagnostics pass. Diagnostics fail closed for missing or duplicate session/channel ids, unsupported transport capabilities, platform socket or native transport handle requests, insecure untrusted remote sessions without encrypted_transport and authenticated_peer requirements, missing replay seeds, zero fixed replay tick rates, missing deterministic simulation acknowledgement, and missing ordered input acknowledgement. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package now exposes selected --require-networking-foundation-policy package evidence through networking_foundation_* counters for reviewed session rows, transport rows, channel rows, replay prerequisite rows, rejected unsafe transport requirements, insecure remote transport diagnostics, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics. This is not a socket or network I/O path, transport middleware, encryption/authentication handshake implementation, rollback/prediction system, matchmaking/NAT traversal implementation, package format change, renderer/RHI path, native handle surface, package mutation, or broad multiplayer readiness claim.

Runtime Network Transport Adapter Facade v1 exposes RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeRequest, RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeResult, RuntimeNetworkPacketDesc, RuntimeNetworkReceivedPacketRow, IRuntimeNetworkTransportAdapter, and execute_runtime_network_loopback_exchange for one reviewed loopback-only exchange through caller-owned adapters. It fails closed for missing or unavailable adapters, unsupported loopback, reliable, or unreliable capabilities, native-handle requests, peer_count != 1, invalid channel counts or channels, payloads over the first-party ceiling, invalid service budgets, and adapter exceptions before dispatch. MK_runtime_network_enet is an optional network-enet/vcpkg adapter proof over ENet loopback only. This is not external network readiness, socket ownership for generated games, encryption/authentication, matchmaking/NAT traversal, replication, rollback, package mutation, native handle exposure, or broad multiplayer readiness.

Runtime Network Production Security Gate v1 exposes RuntimeNetworkProductionThreatModelEvidenceRow, RuntimeNetworkProductionValidationEvidenceRow, RuntimeNetworkUnsupportedOnlineClaimRow, RuntimeNetworkProductionSecurityRequest, RuntimeNetworkProductionSecurityPlan, RuntimeNetworkProductionSecurityDiagnosticCode, and plan_runtime_network_production_security_gate for value-only review of threat-modeled networking evidence. The gate consumes already-reviewed networking foundation, production replication, and loopback exchange results, requires sequence/replay rejection, input command validation, snapshot validation, and rollback-window diagnostic evidence, returns host_evidence_required when only loopback/replication host proof is missing, rejects unsupported encryption/authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, cloud service, internet execution, and broad readiness claims, and records zero external network/thread/save/world side effects. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-gameplay-systems emits network_production_security_* counters with reviewed threat-model evidence, host-gated loopback/replication readiness, zero unsupported online claims, and a positive replay hash. This is not secure transport, peer authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, cloud backend services, internet-facing networking, rollback execution, native handle access, or broad online readiness.

Runtime Simulation Orchestration v1 is a host-independent value contract for replay-ready generated gameplay loops: RuntimeSimulationOrchestrationRequest carries simulation_id, next_tick, fixed_tick_rate_hz, accumulated_time_us, max_steps_per_frame, and caller-owned RuntimeSimulationInputCommandDesc rows; plan_runtime_simulation_orchestration returns deterministic fixed-step rows, ordered input-command playback rows, available/planned step counts, consumed/remaining time, and budget_limited status only when diagnostics pass. Diagnostics fail closed for missing simulation ids, invalid fixed tick rates, invalid max steps per frame, missing or duplicate command ids, missing action ids, commands before next_tick, and commands beyond the planned window. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package now exposes selected --require-simulation-orchestration package evidence through simulation_orchestration_* counters for planned fixed-step rows, ordered command playback rows, budget-limited frames, invalid command diagnostics, consumed/remaining time, and clean reviewed-loop diagnostics. This is not a game-rule scheduler, renderer frame pacing system, networking rollback or lockstep layer, package mutation path, editor runtime host, background worker, platform timing API, native handle surface, or broad deterministic multiplayer readiness claim.

Production Authoring Workflows v1 exposes ProductionAuthoringWorkflowRequest, ProductionAuthoringWorkflowRow, ProductionAuthoringWorkflowReviewResult, and review_production_authoring_workflow so reviewed authoring hosts can validate scene placement, quest/dialogue, item/economy, AI behavior, world region, and validation repair workflow rows before game-owned edits or operator validation. The review returns accepted workflow rows, mutation ledger rows, validation repair rows, and diagnostics only; selected sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-production-authoring-workflows smoke emits production_authoring_workflow_* counters for six accepted workflows, six mutation-ledger rows, one validation-repair row, fail-closed shared-surface/arbitrary-shell/cooked-package/native-backend/non-game-target diagnostics, and zero invoked file mutation, package IO, or command execution. This is value-only review metadata, not file mutation, cooked package mutation, arbitrary shell execution, editor action execution, renderer/RHI work, parser/compiler/native handle exposure, autonomous content generation, or broad production authoring automation.

Runtime UI Workbench Production v1 exposes RuntimeUiWorkbenchDocument, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPanelRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTableRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchGraphSeries, RuntimeUiWorkbenchItemRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTextInputFieldRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchFocusPlan, RuntimeUiWorkbenchLocalizationRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchAccessibilityRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPlan, and plan_runtime_ui_workbench so generated games can review dense menu, inventory, equipment, shop, simulation dashboard, table, graph, focus, text-input, localization, and accessibility intent rows before adapter work. The planner returns deterministic rows only when diagnostics pass and fail-closed diagnostics cover duplicate ids, empty or colliding graph/item/text/focus ids, unknown panel/table/focus references, invalid graph points, invalid item/equipment/shop/text-input rows, missing localization/accessibility metadata, and bounded backend/native/editor/RHI/UI-middleware tokens. Selected sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-runtime-ui-workbench smoke emits runtime_ui_workbench_* counters for five panels, three table columns, two table rows, two graph series, three item rows, inventory/equipment/shop rows, one text-input row, one platform text-input request, four focus edges, seventeen localization refs, localization identity readiness, eleven accessibility refs, accessibility identity readiness, zero renderer/text-shaping/font-rasterization/IME/accessibility-bridge/image-decoding/native-platform adapter invocations, and clean diagnostics. This is value-only UI intent and package evidence, not renderer submission, text shaping, font rasterization, IME session execution, OS accessibility publication, image decoding, renderer texture upload, native handle exposure, Dear ImGui/SDL3/UI middleware coupling, or broad production text/font/accessibility readiness.

Asset Placeholder Generation v1 exposes plan_placeholder_asset_bundle and plan_placeholder_asset_cook_package through placeholder_asset_tool.hpp so reviewed engine tooling can derive deterministic first-party placeholder texture, mesh, material, and audio source documents, synchronized GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 content, changed-file hashes, provenance rows, and registered source cook/package planning through PlaceholderAssetBundleRequest, PlaceholderAssetBundlePlan, PlaceholderAssetCookPackageRequest, PlaceholderAssetCookPackagePlan, PlaceholderAssetChangedFile, PlaceholderAssetProvenanceRow, and PlaceholderAssetDiagnostic. Selected replacement recooks preserve stable AssetKeyV2 rows, source revisions, content hashes, provenance/license rows, existing source registry/package index handoff, and package handoff counters through game.agent.json placeholderAssetPipeline replacementWorkflow rows for sprite, mesh, material, audio, UI, and scene-prop placeholder coverage. This is not an external asset downloader, image/audio generation service, broad source importer execution path, renderer/RHI residency path, package streaming path, game-specific art direction system, native handle surface, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Sprite Atlas Authoring v1 exposes SpriteAtlasSourcePivot, SpriteAtlasSourceSliceBorder, SpriteAtlasSourcePagePolicyDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceFrameDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringPlan, SpriteAtlasSourceChangedFile, SpriteAtlasSourceFrameRow, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDiagnostic, and plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring through sprite_atlas_tool.hpp so reviewed generated 2D host/tooling workflows can validate already-decoded RGBA8 source frames, canonicalize frames by frame id, enforce a selected single-page-tight-rgba8-texture-source page policy, pack through deterministic-sprite-atlas-rgba8-max-side, emit GameEngine.TextureSource.v1 atlas source content, synchronize one GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 texture row, and return changed-file hashes plus frame page id/index, UV, pivot, slice-border, and provenance rows. Sprite Batching Renderer v1 exposes SpriteBatchBudgetLane, SpriteBatchBudgetProfileStatus, SpriteBatchBudgetDiagnosticCode, SpriteBatchBudgetDesc, SpriteBatchBudgetLanePlanDesc, SpriteBatchBudgetRow, SpriteBatchBudgetDiagnostic, SpriteBatchBudgetProfile, and plan_sprite_batch_budget_profile so reviewed renderer/gameplay workflows can evaluate world, UI, and effects lane budgets over existing SpriteBatchPlan rows. Runtime visible-cell sampling and editor package diagnostics are implemented through the later tilemap runtime UX slice; runtime image decoding and production atlas/editor/runtime integration beyond the reviewed source-image decode, atlas-packing tools, and narrow runtime diagnostics, full tilemap editor UX, native GPU sprite batching, package streaming execution, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, and renderer quality remain unsupported or host-gated.

packageSurfaces.notes: Default source-tree build/test/sample lane for headless generated games and engine validation.
Manifest-derived config-only package scaffold with NullRenderer fallback.
Manifest-derived cooked scene package scaffold with runtime package load and renderer-neutral scene submission.

Generated material/shader scaffold with D3D12 host-built shader artifacts and shader_generation_cache_* counters on supported Windows hosts; the strict Vulkan variant proves packaged SPIR-V artifacts plus spirv-val-backed cache/provenance rows only when explicitly gated and does not claim Vulkan renderer residency.
Android package templates and host validation exist on configured hosts, but generated games must run mobile-check and selected Android package commands before claiming readiness.
iOS and Metal readiness requires macOS/Xcode/Apple validation and must remain gated on non-Apple hosts.

physicsBackendAdapterDecisions.decision: Physics 1.0 ready-surface work remains first-party MK_physics only; the optional physics-jolt lane may build MK_physics_jolt, but generated-game defaults and 1.0 ready claims must not require Jolt, native middleware, or public native physics handles.
physicsBackendAdapterDecisions.futureGate: The optional native-physics-middleware-adapter-v1 lane landed through PR #209 / merge commit ccce45701ff3a361977d6286affd46f0ebc16f0d and owns the vcpkg manifest feature physics-jolt, tools/validate-physics-jolt.ps1, docs/dependencies.md, docs/legal-and-licensing.md, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, first-party facade types, deterministic conversion/replay/budget tests, and fail-closed capability negotiation. Any broader middleware parity, cross-platform determinism claim, generated-game default adoption, or public native-handle interop still requires a new dated plan with benchmark and host evidence.
unsupportedClaims: Jolt runtime integration in the default/generated-game 1.0 ready surface
native physics handles in public gameplay APIs
dependency-free 1.0 ready surface requiring third-party physics

hostGates.notes: Strict clang-tidy compile database availability is ready for the default dev validation path. tools/build.ps1 prepares CMake File API codemodel data before the dev configure/build, tools/validate.ps1 reuses that reply for the check-tidy.ps1 -MaxFiles 1 smoke and then runs test.ps1 -SkipBuild, while standalone tools/check-tidy.ps1 still configures the preset when native compile_commands.json is unavailable. tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official CMake/CTest --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism. The wrapper synthesizes clang-tidy arguments rows for Visual Studio generator builds, supports -Files for targeted changed-file clang-tidy on compile-database source files, and still reports missing CMake or clang-tidy as local tool blockers.
